Untitled

2026-06-11

Source: vignettes/articles/holding-the-data-setion.Rmd
holding-the-data-setion.Rmd

Data: student-level records

Research data.   MIDFIELD [@ohland:midfield:2023] is a database of 2.4M students in four tables (student, course, term, degree) that has been managed since 2023 by the American Society for Engineering Education (ASEE). Contact ASEE for further information.

Practice data.   The midfielddata package provides an anonymized sample of the MIDFIELD database organized in four tables (student, course, term, degree) as shown in Table 1. midfielddata is used throughout these articles to demonstrate how midfieldr is used. However, midfielddata must not be used to draw inferences about program attributes or student experiences. midfielddata is for practice, not research.

Table 1. Student-level records in midfielddata
Table Each row is N students N rows N columns Mb memory
student one student 97,555 97,555 13 17.3
course one student per course per term 97,555 3,289,532 12 324.3
term one student per term 97,555 639,915 13 72.8
degree one student per degree 49,543 49,665 5 5.2

Data dictionaries are documented in ?student, ?course, ?term, and ?degree. If you are new to these data, the best place to start is the midfielddata website:

  • MIDFIELD data structure. An article that describes the structure of the four data tables in midfielddata: number of observations, number and class of variables, representative values, and database keys.

  • Data linked by student ID. An article that examines the variables and some representative values in midfielddata—we take a closer look at the records of individual students across the four data tables.

On this page

Developed by Richard Layton, Russell Long, Matthew Ohland, Marisa Orr, Susan Lord, US National Science Foundation.

Site built with pkgdown 2.2.0.
